title: Referential Integrity
text: Referential Integrity By default, Uniface handles referential integrity checking. For some databases, it is possible for the database itself to handle referential integrity checking. You can enforce the referential integrity of a database in the following ways: Let Uniface handle the referential integrity, so that Uniface keeps the database consistent Let the RDBMS handle the referential integrity. To inform Uniface about the desired method, use uinfo . Note: Referential integrity should be maintained either in Uniface, or in the database. Implementing referential integrity in both Uniface and the database has a negative impact on performance. DBMS connectors that support referential integrity in the database should support a connector option to disable or enable Uniface referential integrity. If an application uses multiple DBMS connectors, all DBMS connectors must be configured to use Uniface referential integrity support. This is because every DBMS connector enables or disables the Uniface implementation of referential integrity for all tables (including Uniface system tables) accessed by that connector. Related Links Referential Integrity Referential integrity is a standard feature of relational database design that prevents users or applications from entering inconsistent data. Most relational databases have referential integrity rules that you can apply when you create a relationship between tables. uinfo Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector.
